Ye structures memory repositories hain — har crystal ka shard storage aur participant dono hai. Ye… consciousness ke fragments hain. Kethari ne apne aap ko yahan preserve kiya.”

Kieran stiffen hua. “Tum kahte ho — jo hamare around hai, wo wohi hain? Millions of them?”

“Haan,” Atheus ne kaha. “Lekin fractured. Wo poore nahi hain. Har shard ek thought, ek memory, ek sensation hai. Unhe fully awaken karne ke liye unification required hogi.”

Mira ne brow皱di. “Aur exactly wahi cheez Orelon chahte hain.”

Atheus ne sar jhukaya. “Orelon believe karte hain ki unification unhe Kethari ki complete technological inheritance degi. Lekin process unstable hai. Zyada force, aur shards chaos mein collapse ho sakte hain. Kethari ko awaken karne ki bajaye, wo reality ko fracture kar sakte hain.”

Achanak city se ek vibration guzra, pehle se zyada deep. Crystalline spires sway hue, reeds ki tarah bend hue. Unke core par, skyline ko split karke ek fissure of light appear hua — pulsing, widening, aur violet fire se crackling.

“The Quantum Fracture,” Atheus ne whisper kiya. “Unhone already shuru kar diya hai.”

Mira aur Kieran run karne lage, shifting pathways ke dwara guide hue jo unki movement par open aur close ho rahe the. City khud unhe shepherd kar rahi thi, channels of light aage bloom kar rahe the aur peeche seal ho rahe the.

Wo L’Varn ke dil tak pahunche: ek vast amphitheater jo living crystal se carve kiya gaya tha. Uske center ke upar ek lattice of energy suspend thi, stars ke web ki tarah woven, aur uske core par Orelon Construct hover kar raha tha — ab hidden nahi, ab restrained nahi. Uska metallic form blades aur orbs ki spire mein unfold hua, shards se light siphon kar raha tha.

Mira ki saans ruki. “Wo fragments par feed kar rahe hain.”

“Unka intention mere dar se bhi worse hai,” Atheus ne kaha. Uska projection energy intensify hone par waver hua. “Wo sirf Kethari ko awaken nahi kar rahe. Wo fragments ko rewrite kar rahe hain — jo unke design ke liye serve nahi karta, use erase kar rahe hain.”

“Jiska matlab hai Kethari apne aap mein return nahi karenge,” Kieran ne mutter kiya. “Wo Orelon ke roop mein return karenge.”

Construct ki voice boomed, sound nahi balki thought ke roop mein:

“Witness, inheritors. Fragmentation ka age khatam hota hai. Hamare through, unity shuru hoti hai.”

Lattice pulse hui, aur skyline mein fissure widen hui. Mira ki haddiyon mein dard hua jaise uski taraf kheench raha ho. Wo stagger hui, Kieran ke arm ko pakdi. “Wo city ke quantum layers ko destabilize kar rahe hain. Agar ye collapse ho gaya—”

Atheus ne grimly kaha, “—toh fracture spread hoga, matter ko dimensions ke across unseam karega. Ye world, aur isse tied doosre worlds, unravel ho sakte hain.”

Kieran ne apne side se Quantum Blade nikala, weapon fracture ke saath sympathetic vibration se shimmer kar raha tha. “Hum ise yahan rok lenge, warna kabhi nahi rok payenge.”

Mira ne use eye contact kiya. “Together.”

Atheus ne apna form extend kiya, spectral body se threads of light hawa mein flow hue. “Main Archive ki resonance ko counter karne ke liye channel kar sakta hoon, lekin anchor ke bina kaafi nahi hoga. Tum mein se ek ko lattice mein step karna hoga.”

Words jaise ek blow ki tarah lagi.

Kieran ne daant pechhe li. “Tum kahte ho tum mein se ek ko fracture se tie karna hoga. Risk of being shredded.”

Mira ne pulsing lattice ko dekha, heart racing. Wo already uski pull feel kar sakti thi, jaise ye use recognize kar raha ho. Uske chest mein resonance — shard jo usne Thalen ke ruins se carry kiya tha — city ke saath beat kar raha tha.

“Ye mujhe chahta hai,” usne softly kaha.

“No.” Kieran ka grip tight hua. “Tum nahi jaanti ki ye tumhare saath kya karega.”

“Main jaanti hoon,” usne whisper kiya. “Landing ke moment se hi bulaa raha hai. Main ise tab tak hold back kar sakti hoon jab tak tum strike kar sako.”

Construct ki voice phir surge hui, unke thoughts ko drown kar diya:

“Futile resistance. Tum khud fragments ho. Hum inevitability hain.”

Lattice flare hui, aur amphitheater quake hua.

Mira aage badhi. “Phir dekhte hain ki inevitability bleed kar sakti hai ya nahi.”

Usne lattice par haath rakha — aur light erupt hui. City ke shards resonance mein chillaye, billion fractured voices ek saath rone lage. Energy usme pour hui, uski veins ko starlight se sear kar diya, lekin wo firm rahi. Uski presence ne lattice se weave kar liya, uske violent tremor ko steadier, anchored banaya.

Atheus ki voice thundered. “Ab, Kieran!”

Gurrate hue, Kieran ne aage lunged, Quantum Blade high raise kiya. Weapon white burn kar raha tha, uska edge probability ko cut karne ke frequency par vibrate kar raha tha, na ki flesh ko. Usne swing kiya —

Aur Construct ke dil par strike kiya.

Amphitheater light mein detonate hua. Shards meteors ki tarah cascade hue, fracture chillaya jaise universe khud tear ho gaya ho —

Phir silence.

Jab light dim hui, Mira collapse hui, uska body abhi bhi faintly glow kar raha tha. Kieran ne use pakda, heart pounding. Construct gone tha, fragments mein shatter ho gaya jo dying embers ki tarah upward drift kar rahe the. Skyline mein fissure seal ho gayi, sirf horizon par violet ka ek faint scar chhod kar.

Lekin Mira ki aankhen khuli, irises crystalline facets se shimmer kar rahi thi. Uski voice layered aayi, human aur alien:

“They’re not gone. The Kethari are awake.”

Chapter Eighteen: The Voice of Shards

Construct ke collapse ke baad jo silence aayi, wo empty nahi thi. Wo dense, humming thi, jaise storm se pehle ki hawa. Mira ka body faintly crystalline light se pulse kar raha tha, uska chest shallow breaths le raha tha. Kieran use tight pakde hue tha, decide nahi kar pa raha tha ki wo use comfort kar raha hai — ya khud ko brace kar raha hai against what she had become.

Atheus ka projection waver kar raha tha, itni energy channel karne ke baad uska form unstable. Lekin uski voice steady thi. “The Kethari stir kar rahe hain. Ab wo uska anchor hain. Uske through, wo return kar chuke hain.”

Mira ki aankhen poori tarah khuli. Glow ab zyada bright tha, fractal patterns uski irises par dance kar rahe the. Jab usne bola, toh uski words layered aayin, ek chorus uski human voice ke neeche.

“We… remember.”

Kieran stiffen hua. “Mira? Kya… abhi bhi tum ho?”

Uski nazar use hiti — thaki hui, human, lekin kuch vast se infused. “Main hoon,” usne kaha, though voices beneath her whispered in a dozen languages. “Aur zyada. Wo mere andar hain. Nahi — mere saath hain.”

City ne khud respond kiya. Crystalline towers shiver hue, unka resonance dissonance ki bajaye harmony mein align hua. Light ke pathways outward unfurl hue, amphitheater ki taraf converge hue jaise bow kar rahe hon.

Atheus ne sar jhukaya. “Wo tumhe conduit ke roop mein acknowledge kar rahe hain. Fragments tumhari existence ke through unity seek kar rahe hain.”

“Ye plan nahi tha,” Kieran ne mutter kiya, abhi bhi Quantum Blade ko tight pakde hue, knuckles white. “Hum yahan Orelon ko rokne aaye the. Ab wo — kya? Unka vessel?”

“Not vessel,” Atheus ne correct kiya. “Symbiont. Lekin danger abhi bhi hai. Symbiosis heal kar sakti hai — ya consume. Balance tumhari will par depend karega.”

Mira ne aankhen band kar li, andar ki voices par focus kiya. Memories jo uski nahi thi, usme flood hue — images of civilization spanning stars, thought se woven crystalline bodies, unki perfect unity jab tak ambition ne unhe fracture nahi kar diya. Usne unki creation ki joy, collapse ki grief, betrayal ki rage mehsoos ki.

Aur kuch aur.

“Wo dar rahe hain,” usne whisper kiya. “The Kethari… wo Orelon ko yaad karte hain. Wo jaante hain ki ye khatam nahi hua.”

Amphitheater se ek tremor roll hua. Upar, horizon par violet ka scar brighten hua. Andar se, kuch writhing kar raha tha.

Kieran ne curse kiya. “Wo phir se aa rahe hain?”

“No,” Mira ne kaha, voice steady lekin strained. “Not the Construct. Worse. Orelon ne ek se zyada seed kiye. Wo fracture unka only tether nahi tha. Unhone redundancies across systems prepare ki thi. Agar ek fail hota, toh doosre activate ho jate.”

Atheus ki aankhen spectral fire se flare hui. “Phir war contained nahi hai. Orelon apni corruption har shard tak spread karenge jo wo dhundh lenge.”

Kieran ne Mira ki taraf dekha. “Phir hum kya karenge? Hum unhe kaise rok sakte hain bina tumhe alive burn kiye?”

Mira slowly uthi, uske movements graceful, inhuman. Uske veins mein flow karne wali light patterns mein stabilize hui, skin ke neeche constellations ki tarah glow kar rahi thi. Ek pal ke liye, Kieran ne use fragile nahi, balki formidable dekha — do species ka fused embodiment.

“Hum doosre fractures dhundhenge,” usne kaha. “Aur unhe heal karenge pehle ki Orelon kar paye. Kethari humein guide kar sakte hain — mere through.”

Atheus ne use study kiya. “Ye tumhe tear apart kar dega, human. Har tether jo tum confront karo, zyada resonance demand karega. Eventually, tumhari individuality dissolve ho sakti hai.”

Mira ne Kieran ki aankhon mein dekha, voice quiet lekin resolute. “Agar iska matlab hai unhe rokna, toh main risk karungi.”

Kieran ne sar hila diya, anger rise hua. “Tum phir se apne aap ko sacrifice mat banao. Hum ek doosra tareeka dhundhenge.”

Usne uska haath pakda, uski fingers glow ke despite warm thi. “Kieran… shayad doosra tareeka nahi hai. Lekin agar hai, toh hum use together hi dhundh payenge. Mujhe wada karo ki tum mujhe akeli face nahi karne doge.”

Uska throat tighten hua, lekin usne sar hila diya. “Together. Always.”

City vibrate hui, applause jaisi resonance. Pathways ek ek karke light hue, outward point kar rahe the, horizon ke beyond, jahan doosre fractures void mein faintly pulse kar rahe the.

Mira ne mudkar dekha, uski voice ab determination aur alien harmony ka blend thi.

“Phir chalo khatam karte hain jo Orelon ne shuru kiya. Apne terms par.”

L’Varn ke towers bend hue, sky mein light ka corridor banane ke liye align hue, ek gateway. Kethari fragments khud hi path khole rahe the.

Kieran ne blade ko tight pakda. Atheus ne apna projection steady kiya. Mira, shared consciousness se glowing, pehle step liya.

Aur together, wo corridor mein vanish hue, resurrected city ko apne peeche humming chhod kar.

Chapter Nineteen: Across the Fractures

Light ka corridor unhe stars ke beech ke void mein phenk diya. Koi ship, koi cockpit, koi hull — bas teeno suspended the, ek river of energy mein jo space ko apne around bend kar rahi thi. Quantum Blade Kieran ke side par hum kar raha tha, passage ke saath resonate kar raha tha jaise ye strange medium ko recognize kar raha ho.

Mira front par float kar rahi thi, uska body faintly glow kar raha tha. Fractal constellations uski skin ke neeche ripple kar rahe the, shifting currents ke saath rhythm mein flicker kar rahe the. Kieran ke liye, wo jis aurat ko wo jaanta tha usse kam, ek star in human form jaisi lag rahi thi.

Atheus calm tha, though uska projection edges par waver kar raha tha. “Ye sirf ek tunnel nahi hai. Ye Kethari Path hai — ek network jo physical spacetime se beyond hai. Har fracture yahan resonate karti hai, points of light ki tarah. Mira ke through, hum un tak directly pahunchna kar sakte hain.”

Kieran ne brow皱di. “Aur kya hoga agar wo pehle fracture tak pahunchna se pehle burn out ho jaye?”

Mira ne apna sar mudaya, uski aankhen crystal ke twin shards ki tarah glow kar rahi thi. “Phir tum wahi karo jo maine shuru kiya.”

“No,” Kieran snap kiya. “Even think mat karo aise. Tum sirf koi… bridge nahi ho jisse cross kiya jaye. Tum tum ho. Aur main tumhe unke liye lose nahi kar raha.”

Uski lips sad smile mein curve hui. “Tumhe choose karne ka mauka nahi milega.”

Jab tak wo reply kar pata, corridor violently shift hua. Aage, violet ka jagged scar reality mein wound ki tarah pulse kar raha tha. Jitna closer wo pahunche, utna zyada Kieran ko apni haddiyon mein dard hua, jaise uske atoms hi resonance ke against resist kar rahe hon.

“Second fracture,” Atheus ne grimly kaha. “Unstable. Orelon already at work hain.”

Corridor tear hua, aur wo — perpetual dusk ki ek world mein gir gaye.

Sky fractured thi, black aur violet ke shards broken glass ki tarah interlock kar rahe the. Unke neeche ek dead ocean stretch kar raha tha, uska surface glassy aur cracked. Cracks se streams of light seep kar rahi thi, reverse waterfalls ki tarah hawa mein rise kar rahi thi.

Sabke center mein ek metal aur shadow ki spire looming thi: ek aur Orelon Construct, pehle se bhi bada, upar ke fracture se tethered. Uska surface stolen Kethari light se pulse kar raha tha, use jagged streams of power mein twist kar raha tha.

Kieran ne khud ko steady kiya, blade pakda. “L’Varn se worse lag raha hai.”

Mira slightly sway hui, uska glow flicker kar raha tha. “Kyunki hai. Ye fracture sirf opening nahi hai. Ye spread ho rahi hai. Agar ise contain nahi kiya gaya, toh ye puri system ko consume kar legi.”

“Aur tum suppose kar rahi ho ki tum ise akeli rokogi?” Kieran demanded.

Atheus ka projection closer hover hua, uski voice grave. “Nahi. Wo resonance ko pehle ki tarah anchor karegi — lekin iss baar, tumhe zyada karna hoga. Construct bada hai, reinforced. Use sever karne ke liye, tumhe simultaneously multiple points par strike karna hoga. Akela, tum nahi kar sakte.”

“Phir tum kya suggest karte ho?”

Atheus ne spectral hands extend kiye. “Join with me. Mere consciousness ko apne andar aane do. Together, hum blade ki probability strike ko multiply kar sakte hain. Lekin easy nahi hoga — tumhara dimaag overlap withstand nahi kar payega.”

Kieran ne unke beech dekha — Mira alien shards se glow kar rahi, Atheus ghostly fire se shimmer kar raha. Usne bitter laugh liya. “Toh mere choices hain: apni sanity lose karo, ya universe lose karo. Great odds.”

Mira ne use pakda, uska touch steady. “Tum akela nahi hoge. Main tumhe anchor karungi. Kethari fragments overlap ko stabilize kar sakte hain. Lekin tumhe mujhpar trust karna hoga.”

Usne uski glowing eyes mein dekha, alien light jo slowly us aurat ko replace kar raha tha jo wo jaanta tha. Trust her. Even if part of her wasn’t hers anymore.

Usne hard swallow kiya. “Alright. Chalo karte hain.”

Wo ek saath spire ki taraf badhe. Construct ne mudkar dekha, uske jagged limbs unfold hue, aur Orelon ki voice fractured sky mein boomed:

“Inheritors no longer. Tum inevitability ke against resistance ho. Tum anomaly ho. Tum unmade ho jaaoge.”

Mira aage badhi, uska glow surge hua. “Humne pehle bhi suna hai.”

Usne haath uthaye, aur ocean ke shards glass ki army ki tarah uth gaye. Light unme se bleed hui, spire ko wrap karne ke liye lattice mein weave hui, use place mein hold kar rahi thi. Billion Kethari voices ki resonance usme thunder kar rahi thi.

Kieran ne feel kiya Quantum Blade uske grip mein tremble kar raha hai jab Atheus ka consciousness usme pour hua. Hazaron memories, hazaron lifetimes ka knowledge, sab uske dimaag mein crash hue. Uska vision blur hua, layers mein split hua — wo khud ko strike, fail, survive, die karte hue dekh raha tha. Uska body convulse hua, lekin Mira ka glow ne use steady kiya, uski existence ko anchor kiya.

“Kieran!” Mira ki layered voice storm ke through cut kar gayi. “Ab — strike!”

Gurrate hue, wo aage surge hua, blade infinite echoes mein split hua, har ek different probability par vibrate kar raha tha. Usne ek baar — do baar — teen baar — Construct ke har weak point par strike kiya, uske strikes ke echoes ek impossible blow mein collapse hue.

Spire shatter hua.

Upar ka fracture chillaya, uska violet light convulse hua. Ek pal ke liye, world tear apart hone laga — phir Mira ne apne haath higher uthaye, uska body white fire se blaze kar raha tha. Ocean ke shards uth kar wound ko seal kar diye, jaise glass flame ke under fuse ho raha ho.

Silence.

Kieran knees par collapse hua, gasping, uska dimaag echoes se ringing jo uska nahi tha. Atheus ka projection violently flicker kar raha tha, barely holding form.

Aur Mira —

Mira abhi bhi khadi thi. Lekin uska body sirf glowing nahi tha. Wo translucent tha, crystalline veins uski skin par spread kar rahe the. Uski voice, jab usne bola, sirf layered nahi thi — multiplied thi, ek chorus.

“We are holding. Lekin fractures abhi bhi hain. Bahut. Bahut zyada.”

Kieran ne upar dekha, uska chest tighten hua. Wo abhi bhi Mira thi. Aur phir bhi, already, wo kuch aur thi.

“How long,” usne whisper kiya, “before you’re not you anymore?”

Mira ki crystalline eyes soften hui. “Jitna bhi lage isse khatam karne mein.”

Chapter Twenty: The Last Fracture

Kethari Path violently bend hui, uske once-glowing strands frayed hone ki tarah flicker kar rahe the. Mira ne unhe forward guide kiya, uska body crystalline beacon ki tarah shimmer kar raha tha. Kieran uske saath stagger kar raha tha, uska dimaag abhi bhi Atheus ke saath fusion se ache kar raha tha. Archivist ka projection ab dimensions ke across thinly stretched ek faint shimmer se zyada nahi tha.

Atheus ki voice weary lekin resolute thi. “Last tether aage hai. Orelon ne ise void between systems mein anchor kiya hai, kisi single world se beyond. Ye unka core hai — agar hum ise sever karte hain, toh unka pura network collapse ho jayega.”

Kieran ne Quantum Blade ko tight pakda. “Aur agar hum nahi kar paaye?”

“Phir inevitability jeet jayegi,” Mira ne softly kaha. Lekin uski voice ab sirf uski nahi thi. Countless Kethari ki resonance thi, ek harmony mein woven jo Kieran ke dil ko dard de rahi thi.

Path khula — aur wo darkness mein emerge hue.

Space nahi, void nahi, balki kuch deeper. Silence ka ek gulf jahan light bhi exist karne se hesitant lagti thi. Uske center mein Orelon Core Construct hang kar raha tha: vast, labyrinthine, blades aur shifting geometry ka ek black star. Violet fire ke tendrils outward stretch kar rahe the, invisible membranes ko pierce kar rahe the aur countless worlds ke fractures ko anchor kar rahe the.

Kieran ki saans ruki. “Isse fight kaise karenge?”

Mira float kar rahi thi, uska glow intensify ho raha tha. “Hum ise fight nahi karenge. Hum fragments se zyada ban jayenge.”

Uska body pulse hua — aur ek heartbeat ke liye, Kieran ne use light mein dissolve hote dekha, shards ka constellation jo void ke across stretch kar raha tha. Kethari voices usme thunder kar rahi thi, Core ko vibrate kar rahi thi.

“We are memory. We are unity. We are not yours to consume.”

Construct ne respond kiya, uski voice unshakable finality ke saath boomed:

“Unity hamari hai. Tum anomaly ho. Tum rewritten ho jaaoge.”

Tendrils lashed out, violet arcs void ko tear kar rahe the. Kieran ne blade uthaya, ek ko slice kar diya, lekin har strike uske body ko tremble karwa raha tha. Ye koi ordinary fight nahi tha — har blow probabilities ke across resonate kar raha tha, usko existence se unravel karne ki dhamki de raha tha.

“Mira!” usne chillaya. “Tum ise akeli hold nahi kar sakti!”

Uska glow flicker hua, phir steady hua. “Main akeli nahi hoon. Ab nahi.”

Uske being ke shards expand hue, Kieran aur Atheus ke around weave hue. Pehli baar, usne Kethari ko apne andar mehsoos kiya — invasive thoughts nahi, balki allies ke roop mein. Unki resonance ne uske dimaag ko steady kiya, use uski rhythm mein align kiya. Blade ka hum sharpen hua, uska edge white burn kar raha tha.

Atheus ki voice fusion ke dwara strong hui. “Ab hum ek saath strike karenge — human will, archivist memory, Kethari unity. Ek frequency!”

Tino ek saath aage surge hue.

Kieran ne swing kiya, blade infinite echoes mein split hua. Mira ki light ne Construct ko pierce kiya, uske tendrils ko unravel kiya. Atheus ki resonance ne har movement ko guide kiya, unke strike ko collapsing probability ke rhythm mein harmonize kiya.

Core chillaya, uska form convulse hua. Uske surface par violet fractures spiderweb kar gaye.

Lekin kaafi nahi tha.

Construct inward collapse hone laga, sab kuch apne saath kheench raha tha — light, sound, thought. Inevitability ka ek black hole. Agar ye unhe consume kar leta, toh Orelon ka rewriting unchecked spread ho jata.

Mira ne Kieran ki taraf dekha, uski crystalline eyes blazing. Ek pal ke liye, usne sirf use dekha — na Kethari chorus, na alien glow. Bas Mira.

“Kieran,” usne whisper kiya. “Yahan main chhod deti hoon.”

“No,” usne fiercely kaha, uska arm pakda. “Har cheez ke baad nahi. Hum ek doosra tareeka dhundhenge!”

Usne apna haath uske cheek par rakha, light ke despite warm. “Tum doosra tareeka ho. Main unhe itni door tak carry kar chuki hoon. Ab tum mujhe carry karo.”

Jab tak wo protest kar pata, wo aage jhuki, uska forehead uske forehead se touch hua. Light usme se usme pour hui, resonance uski veins mein flood hui. Uska body arched hua, uska dimaag billion voices se blaze kar raha tha — lekin sabke center mein, ek voice thi, soft aur human.

“Don’t forget me.”

Aur phir — wo gayi.

Uska form pure light mein shatter hua, Core se fuse hua. Ek instant ke liye, Construct white blaze kar raha tha, ab violet nahi. Kethari fragments — Mira ke sacrifice ke through — ne Orelon tether ko andar se rewrite kar diya.

Kieran ne chillaya, Quantum Blade ko Construct ke dil mein drive kiya.

Void detonate hua.

Jab light clear hui, Core gone tha. Fractures sealed thi. Void ki silence real stars mein badal gayi — unbroken.

Kieran float kar raha tha, gasping, blade uske haath mein dim. Atheus uske saath flicker kar raha tha, weaker than ever.

“It is done,” Archivist ne softly kaha. “Orelon network toot gaya hai. Unki inevitability… undone.”

Kieran ka throat tighten hua. “Aur Mira?”

Atheus ki nazar unreadable thi. “Wo lost nahi hai. Wo har jagah hai. Kethari phir se whole hain, uski wajah se. Aur unke through… wo endure karegi.”

Kieran ne aankhen band kar li, blade ko apne chest se laga liya. Hum faint thi — lekin usme, usne socha uski voice suni.

“Together. Always.”

Usne aankhen kholi, apne around newborn stars ko dekha. War khatam ho gayi thi. Lekin cost —

Cost kabhi fade nahi hogi.

Epilogue

Years later, distant systems mein crystalline glow ke whispers failne lage, jo lost travelers ko ghar guide kar rahe the. Kuch log ise Kethari ka return kehte the. Doosre ise myth kehte the.

Lekin Kieran ke liye, stars ke beech ghoomte hue, blade apne side par, wo sach jaanta tha.

Mira abhi bhi wahan thi. Watching. Waiting.

Aur jab Orelon phir uthenge — jaise ki surely uthenge —

wo face karenge na fragments, balki unity.
